title: 中考六选五 9: Reading Food Labels
passage:
Many students choose food by taste, price or packaging. A snack with bright colours may look attractive, and a drink may seem healthy because of its name. 1. ______ Reading food labels can help students make better choices.

The first thing to check is the ingredient list. Ingredients are usually listed from the largest amount to the smallest. 2. ______ If sugar appears near the beginning, the food may contain more sugar than expected.

Students should also look at serving size. A bottle of juice may seem to have only a little sugar, but the label may show that the bottle contains two servings. 3. ______ If you drink the whole bottle, you may actually take in twice the amount.

Another useful habit is to be careful with health words. Words like “natural” or “low-fat” can sound good, but they do not always mean the food is healthy. 4. ______ The real information is usually in the details of the label.

Learning to read labels takes time, but it gives students more control over what they eat. 5. ______ Instead of being guided only by advertisements, they can make choices based on information.

questions.label: Questions 1-5 - Sentence Insertion
questions.instruction: Choose the best sentence from A-F for each blank in the passage. There is one extra sentence you do not need.

choices.text: This means the numbers may not describe the whole bottle or package.
However, these things do not always tell us what is inside the food.
The earlier an ingredient appears, the more of it the food usually has.
Students should judge the full label instead of trusting one attractive word.
It helps them become wiser consumers.
Students should stop eating all packaged food.
